title: dianthera campestris
intro: Dianthera campestris, also known as feverfew, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region and other parts of the world. The leaves and flowers of the feverfew plant have been used in herbal medicine for centuries, and they possess medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, as well as the ability to inhibit the release of chemical mediators involved in allergic reactions. The health benefits of feverfew include reducing the frequency and severity of migraine headaches, alleviating symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, and decreasing inflammation in the body. Feverfew is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and it is also available as a dried herb or fresh plant material for use in cooking and herbal remedies.
